haftalar.kazanim: E10.1.1 — Discussing school systems and experiences
haftalar.kazanimDetay: Students compare school systems in different countries using comparative structures.

haftalar.kazanim: E10.1.2 — Giving advice about studying strategies
haftalar.kazanimDetay: Students give advice using should, ought to and could about study habits.
Students use be going to, will and present continuous to describe different types of future plans.
Students use in order to/so that/because to explain purpose and reason in planning contexts.

haftalar.kazanim: E10.3.1 — Describing legendary and historical figures
haftalar.kazanimDetay: Students use past simple and past perfect to narrate the lives of historical figures.
Students write a structured biography using sequencing words and time markers.

haftalar.kazanim: E10.4.1 — Describing cultural traditions and ceremonies
haftalar.kazanimDetay: Students discuss cultural traditions using passive voice and present simple.
Students compare cultural practices using complex comparative structures.

haftalar.kazanim: E10.5.1 — Planning a trip: making reservations
haftalar.kazanimDetay: Students use formal language to make hotel and transport reservations in role-plays.
Students use present perfect and past simple to describe travel experiences.

haftalar.kazanim: E10.6.1 — Writing how-to texts and instructions
haftalar.kazanimDetay: Students write instructional texts using imperative sentences and sequencing language.
Students use must/mustn't, have to/don't have to to give rules and regulations.
Students use modal verbs (might/could/must) to speculate about mysterious phenomena.

haftalar.kazanim: E10.7.2 — Relative clauses in extended descriptions
haftalar.kazanimDetay: Students use defining and non-defining relative clauses to provide detailed descriptions.

haftalar.kazanim: E10.8.1 — Shopping language and consumer rights
haftalar.kazanimDetay: Students use formal complaint language and consumer vocabulary in shopping contexts.
Students use quantifiers and numerical expressions in commercial contexts.
Students discuss sports events and results using present perfect and simple past.
Students use could/was able to/managed to to describe past and present ability.
